meta.name: Wisdom of Solomon
meta.copyright: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.Base: Love righteousness, you rulers of the earth, think of the Lord in goodness and seek him with sincerity of heart;
text.KJV: 1:1 Loue righteousnesse, yee that be iudges of the earth: thinke of the Lord with a good (heart) and in simplicitie of heart seeke him.
text.DRV: 1:1 Love justice, you that judge the earth. Think of our Lord in goodness, and in simplicity of heart seek him:

text.Base: because he is found by those who do not put him to the test, and manifests himself to those who do not distrust him.
text.KJV: 1:2 For hee will bee found of them that tempt him not: and sheweth himselfe vnto such as doe not distrust him.
text.DRV: 1:2 because he is found of them that tempt him not: and he appeareth to them that have faith in him.

text.Base: For perverse thoughts separate people from God, and when his power is tested, it exposes the foolish;
text.KJV: 1:3 For froward thoughts separate from God: and his power when it is tryed, reprooueth the vnwise.
text.DRV: 1:3 For perverse cogitations separate from God: and proved power chasteneth the unwise:

text.Base: because wisdom will not enter a deceitful soul, or dwell in a body enslaved to sin.
text.KJV: 1:4 For into a malitious soule wisedome shall not enter: nor dwell in the body that is subiect vnto sinne.
text.DRV: 1:4 because wisdom will not enter into a malicious soul, nor dwell in a body subject to sins.

text.Base: For a holy and disciplined spirit will flee from deceit, and will leave foolish thoughts behind, and will be ashamed at the approach of unrighteousness.
text.KJV: 1:5 For the holy spirit of discipline will flie deceit, & remoue from thoughts that are without vnderstanding: and will not abide when vnrighteousnesse commeth in.
text.DRV: 1:5 For the Holy Ghost of discipline will fly from him that feigneth, and will withdraw himself from the cogitations that are without understanding, and he shall be chastened of iniquity ensuing.

text.Base: For wisdom is a kindly spirit, but will not free blasphemers from the guilt of their words; because God is witness of their inmost feelings, and a true observer of their hearts, and a hearer of their tongues.
text.KJV: 1:6 For wisedome is a louing spirit: and will not acquite a blasphemour of his words: for God is witnesse of his reines, and a true beholder of his heart, and a hearer of his tongue.
text.DRV: 1:6 For the spirit of wisdom is gentle, and will not deliver the curser from his lips: because God is witness of his reins, and he is a true searcher of his heart, and an hearer of his tongue.

text.Base: Because the spirit of the Lord has filled the world, and that which holds all things together knows what is said,
text.KJV: 1:7 For the spirit of the Lord filleth the world: and that which containeth all things hath knowledge of the voice.
text.DRV: 1:7 Because the Spirit of our Lord hath replenished the whole world: and that which containeth all things, hath the knowledge of voice.

text.Base: therefore those who utter unrighteous things will not escape notice, and justice, when it punishes, will not pass them by.
text.KJV: 1:8 Therefore he that speaketh vnrighteous things, cannot be hid: neither shal vengeance, when it punisheth. passe by him.
text.DRV: 1:8 For this cause he that speaketh unjust things, cannot be hid, neither shall the chastising judgement pass him.

text.Base: Do not invite death by the error of your life, or bring on destruction by the works of your hands;
text.KJV: 1:12 Seeke not death in the errour of your life: and pull not vpon your selues destruction, with the workes of your hands.
text.DRV: 1:12 Zeal not death in the error of your life, neither procure ye perdition by the works of your hands.

text.Base: because God did not make death, and he does not delight in the death of the living.
text.KJV: 1:13 For God made not death: neither hath he pleasure in the destruction of the liuing.
text.DRV: 1:13 Because God made not death, neither doth he rejoice in the perdition of the living.

text.Base: For he created all things so that they might exist; the generative forces of the world are wholesome, and there is no destructive poison in them, and the dominion of Hades is not on earth.
text.KJV: 1:14 For he created all things, that they might haue their being: and the generations of the world were healthfull: and there is no poyson of destruction in them: nor the kingdome of death vpon the earth.
text.DRV: 1:14 For he created all things to be: and he made the nations of the earth to health: and there is no medicine of destruction in them, nor Kingdom of hell in the earth.

text.Base: For righteousness is immortal.
text.KJV: 1:15 For righteousnesse is immortall.
text.DRV: 1:15 For justice is perpetual and immortal.

text.Base: But the ungodly by their words and deeds summoned death; considering him a friend, they pined away and made a covenant with him, because they are fit to belong to his company.
text.KJV: 1:16 But vngodly men with their workes, and words called it to them: for when they thought to haue it their friend, they consumed to nought, and made a couenant with it, because they are worthy to take part with it.
text.DRV: 1:16 But the impious with hands & words have provoked it: and esteeming it a friend, have fallen to decay, and have made covenants with it: because they are worthy to be of the part thereof.

text.Base: For they reasoned unsoundly, saying to themselves, “Short and sorrowful is our life, and there is no remedy when a life comes to its end, and no one has been known to return from Hades.”
text.KJV: 2:1 For the vngodly said, reasoning with themselues, but not aright: Our life is short and tedious, and in the death of a man there is no remedie: neither was there any man knowen to haue returned from the graue.
text.DRV: 2:1 For they have said thinking with themselves not well: Little, and with tediousness is the time of our life: and in the end of a man there is no recovery, and there is none known that hath returned from hell:

text.Base: “For we were born by mere chance, and hereafter we shall be as though we had never been, for the breath in our nostrils is smoke, and reason is a spark kindled by the beating of our hearts;”
text.KJV: 2:2 For wee are borne at all aduenture: & we shalbe heereafter as though we had neuer bene: for the breath in our nostrils is as smoke, and a litle sparke in the mouing of our heart.
text.DRV: 2:2 because of nothing were we born, and after this we shall be as if we had not been: because the breath is a smoke in our nostrils: & speech a spark to move our heart.

text.Base: “Come, therefore, let us enjoy the good things that exist, and make use of the creation to the full as in youth.”
text.KJV: 2:6 Come on therefore, let vs enioy the good things that are present: and let vs speedily vse the creatures like as in youth.
text.DRV: 2:6 Come therefore, and let us enjoy the good things that are, and let us quickly use the creature as in youth.

text.Base: “Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is inconvenient to us and opposes our actions; he reproaches us for sins against the law, and accuses us of sins against our training.”
text.KJV: 2:12 Therefore let vs lye in wait for the righteous: because he is not for our turne, and he is cleane contrary to our doings: he vpbraideth vs with our offending the Law, and obiecteth to our infamy the transgressings of our education.
text.DRV: 2:12 Let us therefore circumvent the just, because he is unprofitable to us, and he is contrary to our works, and reproachfully objecteth unto us the sins of the law, and defameth in us the sins of our discipline.

text.Base: “He professes to have knowledge of God, and calls himself a child of the Lord.”
text.KJV: 2:13 Hee professeth to haue the knowledge of God: and hee calleth himselfe the childe of the Lord.
text.DRV: 2:13 He boasteth that he hath the knowledge of God, and nameth himself the son of God.

text.Base: Thus they reasoned, but they were led astray, for their wickedness blinded them,
text.KJV: 2:21 Such things they did imagine, and were deceiued: for their owne wickednesse hath blinded them.
text.DRV: 2:21 These things have they thought, and have erred: for their malice hath blinded them.

text.Base: and they did not know the secret purposes of God, nor hoped for the wages of holiness, nor discerned the prize for blameless souls.
text.KJV: 2:22 As for the mysteries of God, they knew them not: neither hoped they for the wages of righteousnesse: nor discerned a reward for blamelesse soules.
text.DRV: 2:22 And they have not known the sacraments of God, nor hoped for the reward of justice, nor esteemed the honour of holy souls.

text.Base: for God created us for incorruption, and made us in the image of his own eternity.
text.KJV: 2:23 For God created man to bee immortall, and made him to be an image of his owne eternitie.
text.DRV: 2:23 For God created man incorruptible, and to the image of his own likeness he made him.

text.Base: but through the devil’s envy death entered the world, and those who belong to his company experience it.
text.KJV: 2:24 Neuerthelesse through enuie of the deuill came death into the world: and they that doe holde of his side doe finde it.
text.DRV: 2:24 But by the envy of the devil, death entered into the world:

text.Base: But the souls of the righteous are in the hand of God, and no torment will ever touch them.
text.KJV: 3:1 But the soules of the righteous are in the hand of God, and there shall no torment touch them.
text.DRV: 3:1 But the souls of the just are in the hand of God, and the torment of death shall not touch them.

text.Base: In the eyes of the foolish they seemed to have died, and their departure was thought to be a disaster,
text.KJV: 3:2 In the sight of the vnwise they seemed to die: and their departure is taken for misery,
text.DRV: 3:2 They seemed in the eyes of the unwise to die: and their decease was counted affliction:

text.Base: and their going from us to be their destruction; but they are at peace.
text.KJV: 3:3 And their going from vs to be vtter destruction: but they are in peace.
text.DRV: 3:3 and that which with us is the way, is destruction: but they are in peace.

text.Base: For though in the sight of others they were punished, their hope is full of immortality.
text.KJV: 3:4 For though they bee punished in the sight of men: yet is their hope full of immortalitie.
text.DRV: 3:4 And though before men they suffered torments, their hope is full of immortality.

text.Base: Having been disciplined a little, they will receive great good, because God tested them and found them worthy of himself.
text.KJV: 3:5 And hauing bene a little chastised, they shalbe greatly rewarded: for God proued them, and found them worthy for himselfe.
text.DRV: 3:5 Vexed in few things, in many they shall be well disposed of: because God hath tempted them, and hath found them worthy of himself.

text.Base: Like gold in the furnace he tried them, and like a sacrificial burnt offering he accepted them.
text.KJV: 3:6 As gold in the furnace hath hee tried them, and receiued them as a burnt offering.
text.DRV: 3:6 As gold in the furnace he hath proved them, and as an host of holocaust he hath received them, & in time there shall be respect of them.

text.Base: In the time of their visitation they will shine forth, and will run like sparks through the stubble.
text.KJV: 3:7 And in the time of their visitation, they shall shine and runne to and fro, like sparkes among the stubble.
text.DRV: 3:7 The just shall shine, and as sparks in a place of reeds they shall run abroad.

text.Base: They will govern nations and rule over peoples, and the Lord will reign over them forever.
text.KJV: 3:8 They shall iudge the nations, and haue dominion ouer the people, and their Lord shall raigne for euer.
text.DRV: 3:8 They shall judge nations, & have dominion over peoples, and their Lord shall reign for ever.

text.Base: Those who trust in him will understand truth, and the faithful will abide with him in love, because grace and mercy are upon his holy ones, and he watches over his elect.
text.KJV: 3:9 They that put their trust in him, shall vnderstand the trueth: and such as be faithfull in loue, shall abide with him: for grace & mercy is to his saints, and he hath care for his elect.
text.DRV: 3:9 They that trust in him, shall understand truth: & the faithful in love shall rest in him: because rest and peace is to his elect.

text.Base: Better is childlessness with virtue, for in the memory of virtue is immortality, because it is known both by God and by mortals.
text.KJV: 4:1 Better it is to haue no children, and to haue vertue: for the memoriall thereof is immortal: because it is knowen with God and with men.
text.DRV: 4:1 Ohow beautiful is the chaste generation with glory! For the memory thereof is immortal: because it is known both with God and with men.

text.Base: But the righteous, though they die early, will be at rest.
text.KJV: 4:7 But though the righteous be preuented with death: yet shal he be in rest.
text.DRV: 4:7 But the just if he be prevented with death, shall be in a place of refreshing.

text.Base: For old age is not honored for length of time, or measured by number of years;
text.KJV: 4:8 For honourable age is not that which standeth in length of time, nor that is measured by number of yeeres.
text.DRV: 4:8 For venerable old age is not that of long time, nor accounted by the number of years: but the understanding of a man are grey hairs:

text.Base: but understanding is gray hair for anyone, and a blameless life is ripe old age.
text.KJV: 4:9 But wisedome is the gray haire vnto men, & an vnspotted life is old age.
text.DRV: 4:9 and an immaculate life is old age.

text.Base: There were some who pleased God and were loved by him, and while living among sinners were taken up.
text.KJV: 4:10 He pleased God, and was beloued of him: so that liuing amongst sinners, he was translated.
text.DRV: 4:10 Pleasing God he is made beloved, and living among sinners he was translated.

text.Base: They were caught up so that evil might not change their understanding or guile deceive their souls.
text.KJV: 4:11 Yea, speedily was he taken away, lest that wickednes should alter his vnderstanding, or deceit beguile his soule.
text.DRV: 4:11 He was taken away lest malice should change his understanding, or lest any guile might deceive his soul.

text.Base: Then the righteous will stand with great confidence in the presence of those who have oppressed them and those who make light of their labors.
text.KJV: 5:1 Then shal the righteous man stand in great boldnesse, before the face of such as haue afflicted him, and made no account of his labours.
text.DRV: 5:1 Then shall the just stand in great constancy against those that have afflicted them, and taken away their labours.

text.Base: When the unrighteous see them, they will be shaken with dreadful fear, and they will be amazed at the unexpected salvation of the righteous.
text.KJV: 5:2 When they see it, they shalbe troubled with terrible feare, & shall be amazed at the strangenesse of his saluation, so farre beyond all that they looked for.
text.DRV: 5:2 They seeing shall be troubled with horrible fear, and shall marvel at the suddenness of unexpected salvation,

text.Base: “We fools thought their life a madness and their end to be without honor.”
text.KJV: 5:4 We fooles accounted his life madnes, and his end to be without honour.
text.DRV: 5:4 We senseless esteemed their life madness, and their end without honour.

text.Base: “How have they been numbered among the children of God? And their lot is among the saints.”
text.KJV: 5:5 How is hee numbred among the children of God, and his lot is among the Saints?
text.DRV: 5:5 Behold how they are counted among the children of God, and their lot is among the saints.

text.Base: But the righteous live forever, and their reward is with the Lord; the Most High takes care of them.
text.KJV: 5:15 But þ righteous liue for euermore, their reward also is with the Lord, and the care of them is with the most High.
text.DRV: 5:15 because the hope of the impious is as dust, which is taken away with the wind: and as a thin froth, which is dispersed by the storm: and as smoke that is scattered abroad by the wind: and as the memory of a guest of one day that passeth.

text.Base: Therefore they will receive a glorious crown and a beautiful diadem from the hand of the Lord, because with his right hand he will cover them, and with his arm he will shield them.
text.KJV: 5:16 Therfore shall they receiue a glorious kingdome, & a beautiful crowne from the Lords hande: for with his right hand shall he couer them, and with his arme shall he protect them.
text.DRV: 5:16 But the just shall live for ever, and their reward is with our Lord, and cogitation of them with the Highest.

text.Base: Listen therefore, O kings, and understand; learn, O judges of the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 6:1 Heare therefore, O yee kings, and vnderstand, learne yee that be iudges of the ends of the earth.
text.DRV: 6:1 Wisdom is better than strength: and a wise man than a strong.

text.Base: For your dominion was given you from the Lord, and your sovereignty from the Most High; he will search out your works and inquire into your plans.
text.KJV: 6:3 For power is giuen you of the Lord, & soueraigntie from the Highest, who shall try your workes; and search out your counsels.
text.DRV: 6:3 Give ear ye that rule multitudes, and that please yourselves in multitudes of nations:

text.Base: Wisdom is radiant and unfading, and she is easily discerned by those who love her, and is found by those who seek her.
text.KJV: 6:12 Wisedome is glorious and neuer fadeth away: yea she is easily seene of them that loue her, and found of such as seeke her.
text.DRV: 6:12 Covet ye therefore my words, and love them, and you shall have discipline.

text.Base: The beginning of wisdom is the most sincere desire for instruction, and concern for instruction is love of her,
text.KJV: 6:17 For the very true beginning of her, is the desire of discipline, and the care of discipline is loue:
text.DRV: 6:17 Because she goeth about seeking them that be worthy of her, and in the ways she will shew herself to them cheerfully, and in all providence she will meet them.

text.Base: and love of her is the keeping of her laws, and giving heed to her laws is assurance of incorruption,
text.KJV: 6:18 And loue is the keeping of her lawes; and the giuing heed vnto her lawes, is the assurance of incorruption.
text.DRV: 6:18 For the beginning of her is the most true desire of discipline.

text.Base: and incorruption brings one near to God;
text.KJV: 6:19 And incorruption maketh vs neere vnto God.
text.DRV: 6:19 The care therefore of discipline, is love: and love is keeping of her laws: and the keeping of the laws, is the consummation of incorruption:

text.Base: so the desire for wisdom leads to a kingdom.
text.KJV: 6:20 Therefore the desire of wisedome bringeth to a kingdome.
text.DRV: 6:20 and incorruption maketh to be next to God.

text.Base: The multitude of the wise is the salvation of the world, and a sensible king is the stability of any people.
text.KJV: 6:24 But the multitude of the wise is the welfare of the world: and a wise king is the vpholding of the people.
text.DRV: 6:24 But what wisdom is, and how she was made, I will declare: and I will not hide from you the mysteries of God, but from the beginning of her nativity I will search out, and set the knowledge of her into light, and will not let pass the truth:

text.Base: I also am mortal, like everyone else, a descendant of the first-formed child of earth; and in the womb of a mother I was molded into flesh,
text.KJV: 7:1 I my selfe also am a mortall man, like to all, and the ofspring of him that was first made of the earth,
text.DRV: 7:1 I also certes am a mortal man, like to all, and of the earthly kindred of him that was made first, and in the womb of my mother was I fashioned flesh,

text.Base: Therefore I prayed, and understanding was given me; I called on God, and the spirit of wisdom came to me.
text.KJV: 7:7 Wherefore I prayed, and vnderstanding was giuen mee: I called vpon God, and the spirit of wisedome came to me.
text.DRV: 7:7 For this cause I wished, and understanding was given me: and I invocated, and the spirit of wisdom came upon me:

text.Base: I preferred her to scepters and thrones, and I accounted wealth as nothing in comparison with her.
text.KJV: 7:8 I preferred her before scepters, and thrones, and esteemed riches nothing in comparison of her.
text.DRV: 7:8 and I preferred her before Kingdoms and thrones, and riches I counted to be nothing in comparison of her.

text.Base: For wisdom, the fashioner of all things, taught me. There is in her a spirit that is intelligent, holy, unique, manifold, subtle, mobile, clear, unpolluted, distinct, invulnerable, loving the good, keen, irresistible,
text.KJV: 7:22 For wisedome which is the worker of all things, taught mee: for in her is an vnderstanding spirit holy, one onely, manifold, subtile, liuely, cleare, vndefiled, plaine, not subiect to hurt, louing the thing that is good, quicke, which cānot be letted, ready to do good:
text.DRV: 7:22 For in her is the spirit of understanding, holy, only, manifold, subtile, eloquent, moveable, undefiled, sure, sweet, loving, good, sharp, who nothing hindereth well-doing,

text.Base: For wisdom is more mobile than any motion; because of her pureness she pervades and penetrates all things.
text.KJV: 7:24 For wisedome is more moouing then any motion: she passeth and goeth through all things by reason of her purenesse.
text.DRV: 7:24 For wisdom is more moveable than all moveable things: and reacheth every-where because of her cleanness.

text.Base: For she is a breath of the power of God, and a pure emanation of the glory of the Almighty; therefore nothing defiled gains entrance into her.
text.KJV: 7:25 For she is the breath of the power of God, and a pure influence flowing from the glory of the Almighty: therefore can no vndefiled thing fall into her.
text.DRV: 7:25 For she is a vapour of the power of God, & a certain sincere emanation of the glory of God omnipotent: and therefore no defiled thing cometh unto her.

text.Base: For she is a reflection of eternal light, a spotless mirror of the working of God, and an image of his goodness.
text.KJV: 7:26 For shee is the brightnesse of the euerlasting light: the vnspotted mirrour of the power of God, and the Image of his goodnesse.
text.DRV: 7:26 For she is the brightness of eternal light, & the unspotted glass of God's majesty, and the image of his goodness.

text.Base: Although she is but one, she can do all things, and while remaining in herself, she renews all things; in every generation she passes into holy souls and makes them friends of God, and prophets;
text.KJV: 7:27 And being but one she can doe all things: and remayning in her selfe, she maketh all things new: and in all ages entring into holy soules, she maketh them friends of God, & Prophets.
text.DRV: 7:27 And whereas she is one, she can do all things: and permanent in herself she reneweth all things, and by nations transporteth herself into holy souls, she maketh the friends of God, and Prophets.

text.Base: for God loves nothing so much as the person who lives with wisdom.
text.KJV: 7:28 For God loueth none but him, that dwelleth with wisedome.
text.DRV: 7:28 For God loveth none, but him that dwelleth with wisdom.

text.Base: She is more beautiful than the sun, and excels every constellation of the stars. Compared with the light she is found to be superior,
text.KJV: 7:29 For she is more beautiful then the Sunne, and aboue all the order of starres, being compared with the light, she is found before it.
text.DRV: 7:29 For she is more beautiful than the sun, and above all disposition of the stars, being compared to light she is found the first.

text.Base: for it is succeeded by the night, but against wisdom evil does not prevail.
text.KJV: 7:30 For after this commeth night: but vice shall not preuaile against wisdome.
text.DRV: 7:30 For night succeedeth to it, but malice overcometh not wisdom.

text.Base: She reaches mightily from one end of the earth to the other, and she orders all things well.
text.KJV: 8:1 Wisdome reacheth from one ende to another mightily: and sweetly doeth she order all things.
text.DRV: 8:1 She reacheth therefore from end unto end mightily, and disposeth all things sweetly.

text.Base: I loved her and sought her from my youth; I desired to take her for my bride, and became enamored of her beauty.
text.KJV: 8:2 I loued her and sought her out, from my youth I dedesired to make her my spouse, and I was a louer of her beautie.
text.DRV: 8:2 Her have I loved, and have sought her out from my youth, and have sought to take her for my spouse, and I was made a lover of her beauty.

text.Base: And if anyone loves righteousness, her labors are virtues; for she teaches self-control and prudence, justice and courage; nothing in life is more profitable for mortals than these.
text.KJV: 8:7 And if a man loue righteousnesse, her labours are vertues: for she teacheth temperance and prudence: iustice and fortitude, which are such things as men can haue nothing more profitable in their life.
text.DRV: 8:7 And if a man love justice: her labours have great virtues: for she teacheth sobriety, and prudence, and justice, and strength, than the which nothing is more profitable in life to men.

text.Base: “O God of my ancestors and Lord of mercy, who have made all things by your word,”
text.KJV: 9:1 O God of my fathers, and Lord of mercy, who hast made all things with thy word,
text.DRV: 9:1 God of my fathers, and Lord of mercy, which madest all things with thy Word,

text.Base: “and by your wisdom have formed humankind to have dominion over the creatures you have made,”
text.KJV: 9:2 And ordained man through thy wisedome, that he should haue dominion ouer the creatures, which thou hast made,
text.DRV: 9:2 and by thy Wisdom didst appoint man, that he should have dominion of the creature, that was made by thee,

text.Base: “give me the wisdom that sits by your throne, and do not reject me from among your servants.”
text.KJV: 9:4 Giue me wisedome that sitteth by thy Throne, and reiect me not from among thy children:
text.DRV: 9:4 give me wisdom the assistant of thy seats, and repel me not from thy children:

text.Base: “With you is wisdom, she who knows your works and was present when you made the world; she understands what is pleasing in your sight and what is right according to your commandments.”
text.KJV: 9:9 And wisedome was with thee: which knoweth thy workes, and was present when thou madest the world, and knew what was acceptable in thy sight, and right in thy Commaundements.
text.DRV: 9:9 and thy wisdom with thee, which knew thy works, which then also was present when thou madest the round world, and knew what was pleasing to thine eyes, and what was direct in thy precepts.

text.Base: “Send her forth from the holy heavens, and from the throne of your glory send her, that she may labor at my side, and that I may learn what is pleasing to you.”
text.KJV: 9:10 O send her out of thy holy heauens, and from the Throne of thy glory, that being present shee may labour with mee, that I may know what is pleasing vnto thee.
text.DRV: 9:10 Send her from thy holy heavens, and from the seat of thy greatness, that she may be with me, and may labour with me, that I may know what is acceptable with thee:

text.Base: “For who can learn the counsel of God? Or who can discern what the Lord wills?”
text.KJV: 9:13 For what man is hee that can know the counsell of God? or who can thinke what the will of the Lord is?
text.DRV: 9:13 For who of men is able to know the counsel of God? Or who can think what God will?

text.Base: “for a perishable body weighs down the soul, and this earthy tent burdens the thoughtful mind.”
text.KJV: 9:15 For the corruptible body presseth downe the soule, and the earthy tabernacle weigheth downe the minde that museth vpon many things.
text.DRV: 9:15 For the body that is corrupted burdeneth the soul, and the earthly habitation presseth down the understanding that thinketh many things.

text.Base: Wisdom protected the first-formed father of the world, when he alone had been created; she delivered him from his transgression,
text.KJV: 10:1 She preserued the first formed father of the world that was created alone, and brought him out of his fall,
text.DRV: 10:1 She kept him, that was first made of God father of the world, when he was created alone,

text.Base: and gave him strength to rule all things.
text.KJV: 10:2 And gaue him power to rule all things.
text.DRV: 10:2 and she brought him out of his sin, and gave him power to contain all things.

text.Base: When the earth was flooded because of him, wisdom again saved it, steering the righteous man by a paltry piece of wood.
text.KJV: 10:4 For whose cause the earth being drowned with the flood, Wisedome againe preserued it, & directed the course of the righteous, in a piece of wood, of small value.
text.DRV: 10:4 For whose cause, when water destroyed the earth, wisdom healed it again, governing the just by contemptible wood.

text.Base: Wisdom also, when the nations in wicked agreement had been confounded, recognized the righteous man and kept him blameless before God, and held him strong in the face of his compassion for his child.
text.KJV: 10:5 Moreouer, the nations in their wicked conspiracie being confounded, she found out the righteous, and preserued him blamelesse vnto God, and kept him strong against his tender compassion towards his sonne.
text.DRV: 10:5 She even in the consent of wickedness, when the nations had confederated themselves, knew the just, and preserved him without blame to God, and in his son's mercy kept the strong.

text.Base: Wisdom rescued a righteous man when the ungodly were perishing; he escaped the fire that descended on the Five Cities.
text.KJV: 10:6 When the vngodly perished, shee deliuered the righteous man, who fled from the fire which fell downe vpon the fiue cities.
text.DRV: 10:6 She delivered the just, flying from the impious that perished, when the fire came down upon Pentapolis:

text.Base: When a righteous man fled from his brother’s wrath, she guided him on straight paths; she showed him the kingdom of God, and gave him knowledge of holy things.
text.KJV: 10:10 When the righteous fled from his brothers wrath, she guided him in right paths: shewed him the kingdome of God: and gaue him knowledge of holy things, made him rich in his trauailes, and multiplied the fruit of his labours.
text.DRV: 10:10 And the just flying his brother's wrath, she conducted by the right ways, and shewed him the kingdom of God, and gave him the knowledge of the holy, did honest him in labours, and accomplished his labours.

text.Base: Wisdom prospered their works by the hand of a holy prophet.
text.KJV: 11:1 She prospered their works in the hand of the holy Prophet.
text.DRV: 11:1 She directed their works in the hands of a holy Prophet.

text.Base: They journeyed through an uninhabited wilderness, and pitched their tents in untrodden places.
text.KJV: 11:2 They went thorough the wildernesse that was not inhabited, and pitched tents in places where there lay no way.
text.DRV: 11:2 They made a journey through the deserts, that were not inhabited: and in desert places they pitched cottages.

text.Base: For it is always in your power to show great strength, and who can withstand the might of your arm?
text.KJV: 11:21 For thou canst shew thy great strength at all times when thou wilt, and who may withstand the power of thine arme?
text.DRV: 11:21 Yea and without these with one spirit they might have been slain suffering persecution of their own facts, and dispersed by the spirit of thy power: but thou hast disposed all things in measure, and number, and weight.

text.Base: Because the whole world before you is like a speck that tips the scales, and like a drop of morning dew that falls on the ground.
text.KJV: 11:22 For the whole world before thee is as a litle graine of the ballance, yea as a drop of the morning dew that falleth downe vpon the earth.
text.DRV: 11:22 For, to be of great force rested always in thee only: & who shall resist the power of thine arm?

text.Base: But you are merciful to all, for you can do all things, and you overlook people’s sins, so that they may repent.
text.KJV: 11:23 But thou hast mercy vpon all: for thou canst doe all things, and winkest at the sinnes of men: because they should amend.
text.DRV: 11:23 Because as the least weight of the balance, so is the round world before thee, and as a drop of the dew before day, that falleth upon the earth.

text.Base: For you love all things that exist, and detest none of the things that you have made, for you would not have made anything if you had hated it.
text.KJV: 11:24 For thou louest all the things that are, and abhorrest nothing which thou hast made: for neuer wouldest thou haue made any thing, if thou hadst hated it.
text.DRV: 11:24 But thou hast mercy on all, because thou canst do all things, and dissemblest the sins of men for repentance.

text.Base: You spare all things, for they are yours, O Lord, you who love the living.
text.KJV: 11:26 But thou sparest all: for they are thine, O Lord, thou louer of soules.
text.DRV: 11:26 And how could any thing continue, unless thou wouldest? Or be preserved which was not called of thee?

text.Base: For your immortal spirit is in all things.
text.KJV: 12:1 For thine vncorruptible spirit is in all things.
text.DRV: 12:1 Ohow good and sweet is thy spirit o Lord in all!

text.Base: Therefore you correct little by little those who trespass, and you remind and warn them of the things through which they sin, so that they may be freed from wickedness and put their trust in you, O Lord.
text.KJV: 12:2 Therefore chastnest thou them by little, and little, that offend, and warnest them by putting them in remembrance, wherin they haue offended, that leauing their wickednesse they may beleeue on thee O Lord.
text.DRV: 12:2 And therefore those that err, by parts thou doest chastise: and doest admonish, and speak to them, concerning the things wherein they sin: that leaving naughtiness, they may believe in thee o Lord.

text.Base: But judging them little by little you gave them an opportunity to repent, though you were not unaware that their origin was evil and their wickedness inborn.
text.KJV: 12:10 But executing thy iudgements vpon them by little and little, thou gauest them place of repentance, not being ignorant that they were a naughtie generation, and that their malice, was bred in them, and that their cogitation would neuer be changed.
text.DRV: 12:10 but judging by parts thou gavest place of repentance, being not ignorant, that the nation of them is wicked, and their malice natural & that their cogitation could not be changed for ever.

text.Base: For neither is there any god besides you, whose care is for all people, to whom you should prove that you have not judged unjustly.
text.KJV: 12:13 For neither is there any God but thou, that careth for all, to whom thou mightest shew that thy iudgement is not vnright.
text.DRV: 12:13 For there is no other God but thou, who hast care of all, that thou mayst shew that thou doest not give judgement unjustly.

text.Base: But being righteous, you rule all things righteously, deeming it alien to your power to condemn anyone who does not deserve to be punished.
text.KJV: 12:15 For so much then as thou art righteous thy selfe, thou orderest all things righteously: thinking it not agreeable with thy power to condemne him þ hath not deserued to be punished.
text.DRV: 12:15 For so much then as thou art just, thou doest dispose all things justly: thou also esteemest it disagreeable from thy power to condemn him, who ought not to be punished.

text.Base: For your strength is the source of righteousness, and your sovereignty over all causes you to spare all.
text.KJV: 12:16 For thy power is the beginning of righteousnesse, and because thou art the Lord of all, it maketh thee to be gracious vnto all.
text.DRV: 12:16 For thy power is the beginning of justice: and for this that thou art Lord of all, thou makest thyself to spare all.

text.Base: For all people who were ignorant of God were foolish by nature; and they were unable from the good things that are seen to know the one who exists, nor did they recognize the artisan while paying heed to his works;
text.KJV: 13:1 Surely vaine are all men by nature, who are ignorant of God, and could not out of the good things that are seene, know him that is: neither by considering the workes, did they acknowledge the worke-master;
text.DRV: 13:1 But all men be vain, in whom there is not the knowledge of God: and of these good things which are seen they could not understand him that is, neither attending to the works have they agnised who was the workman:

text.Base: but they supposed that either fire or wind or swift air, or the circle of the stars, or turbulent water, or the luminaries of heaven were the gods that rule the world.
text.KJV: 13:2 But deemed either fire, or wind, or the swift aire, or the circle of the stars, or the violent water, or the lights of heauen to be the gods which gouerne the world:
text.DRV: 13:2 but either the fire, or the wind, or the swift air, or a circle of stars, or exceeding much water, or the sun and moon, they thought to be gods, rulers of the world.

text.Base: If through delight in the beauty of these things people assumed them to be gods, let them know how much better than these is their Lord, for the author of beauty created them.
text.KJV: 13:3 With whose beautie, if they being delighted, tooke them to be gods: let them know how much better the Lord of them is; for the first Author of beautie hath created them.
text.DRV: 13:3 With whose beauty if being delighted they thought them gods: let them know how much the Lord of them is more beautiful than they. For the author of beauty made all those things.

text.Base: For from the greatness and beauty of created things comes a corresponding perception of their Creator.
text.KJV: 13:5 For by the greatnesse and beautie of the creatures, proportionably the Maker of them is seene.
text.DRV: 13:5 for by the greatness of the beauty, and of the creature, the Creator of them may be seen, to be known thereby.

text.Base: But miserable, with their hopes set on dead things, are those who give the name “gods” to the works of human hands, gold and silver fashioned with skill.
text.KJV: 13:10 But miserable are they, and in dead things is their hope, who called them gods which are the workes of mens hands, golde and siluer, to shewe arte in, and resemblances of beasts, or a stone good for nothing, the worke of an ancient hand.
text.DRV: 13:10 But they are unhappy, & their hope is among the dead, who have called the works of men's hands gods, gold & silver, the invention of art, and the similitudes of beasts, or an unprofitable stone the work of an old hand.

text.Base: For the idea of making idols was the beginning of fornication, and the invention of them was the corruption of life.
text.KJV: 14:12 For the deuising of idoles was the beginning of spiritual fornication, and the inuention of them the corruption of life.
text.DRV: 14:12 For the beginning of fornication is the devising of idols: and the inventing of them is the corruption of life.

text.Base: for they did not exist from the beginning, nor will they last forever.
text.KJV: 14:13 For neither were they from the beginning, neither shall they be for euer.
text.DRV: 14:13 For neither were they from the beginning, neither shall they be for ever.

text.Base: For the worship of idols not to be named is the beginning and cause and end of every evil.
text.KJV: 14:27 For the worshipping of idoles not to be named, is the beginning, the cause, and the end of all euill.
text.DRV: 14:27 For the worship of idols not to be named, is the cause of all evil, and the beginning and end.

text.Base: But you, our God, are kind and true, patient, and ruling all things in mercy.
text.KJV: 15:1 But thou O God, art gracious and true: long suffering, and in mercy ordering all things.
text.DRV: 15:1 But o thou our God art sweet and true, patient, and disposing all things in mercy.

text.Base: For even if we sin we are yours, knowing your power; but we will not sin, because we know that you acknowledge us as yours.
text.KJV: 15:2 For if we sinne we are thine, knowing thy power: but we will not sinne, knowing that we are counted thine.
text.DRV: 15:2 For if we sin, we are thine, knowing thy greatness: and if we sin not, we know that we are counted with thee.

text.Base: For to know you is complete righteousness, and to know your power is the root of immortality.
text.KJV: 15:3 For to know thee is perfect righteousnesse: yea to know thy power is the roote of immortality.
text.DRV: 15:3 For to know thee, is absolute justice: and to know justice, and thy power, is the root of immortality.

text.Base: For the one who turned toward it was saved, not by the thing that was beheld, but by you, the Savior of all.
text.KJV: 16:7 For hee that turned himselfe towards it, was not saued by the thing that he saw: but by thee that art the sauiour of all.
text.DRV: 16:7 For he that turned to it, was not healed by that which he saw, but by thee the Saviour of all:

text.Base: For neither herb nor poultice cured them, but it was your word, O Lord, that heals all people.
text.KJV: 16:12 For it was neither herbe, nor mollifying plaister that restored them to health: but thy word, O Lord, which healeth all things.
text.DRV: 16:12 For neither herb, nor plaster healed them, but thy word, o Lord, which healeth all things.

text.Base: For you have power over life and death; you lead mortals down to the gates of Hades and back again.
text.KJV: 16:13 For thou hast power of life and death: thou leadest to the gates of hell, and bringest vp againe.
text.DRV: 16:13 For it is thou, o Lord, that hast power of life and death, and bringest down to the gates of death, and fetchest again:

text.Base: Instead of these things you gave your people food of angels, and without their toil you supplied them from heaven with bread ready to eat, providing every pleasure and suited to every taste.
text.KJV: 16:20 In stead whereof thou feddest thine owne people, with Angels food, and didst send them from heauen bread prepared without their labour, able to content euery mans delight, and agreeing to euery taste.
text.DRV: 16:20 For the which things thou didst nourish thy people with the meat of Angels, and bread prepared thou gavest them from heaven without labour, having in it all delectation, and the sweetness of all taste.

text.Base: Great are your judgments and hard to describe; therefore uninstructed souls have gone astray.
text.KJV: 17:1 For great are thy Iudgements, and cannot be expressed: therefore vnnourtured soules haue erred.
text.DRV: 17:1 For thy judgements, o Lord, are great, & thy words inexplicable, for this cause the souls lacking discipline have erred.

text.Base: For wickedness is a cowardly thing, condemned by its own testimony; distressed by conscience, it has always exaggerated the difficulties.
text.KJV: 17:11 For wickednesse condemned by her owne witnesse, is very timorous, and being pressed with conscience, alwayes forecasteth grieuous things.
text.DRV: 17:11 For fear is nothing else but a betraying of the aids of cogitation.

text.Base: For fear is nothing but a surrender of the helps that come from reason.
text.KJV: 17:12 For feare is nothing else, but a betraying of the succours which reason offereth.
text.DRV: 17:12 And whilst inwardly there is less expectation, the greater doth he count the ignorance of that cause which maketh the torment.

text.Base: For while gentle silence enveloped all things, and night in its swift course was now half gone,
text.KJV: 18:14 For while all things were in quiet silence, and that night was in the midst of her swift course,
text.DRV: 18:14 For when quiet silence contained all things, and the night was in the mid-way of her course,

text.Base: your all-powerful word leaped from heaven, from the royal throne, into the midst of the land that was doomed, a stern warrior
text.KJV: 18:15 Thine almighty word leapt downe from heauen, out of thy royall throne, as a fierce man of warre into the midst of a land of destruction,
text.DRV: 18:15 thy omnipotent word sallying out of heaven from the royal seats, lighted as a severe conqueror upon the midst of the land of destruction,

text.Base: carrying the sharp sword of your authentic command, and stood and filled all things with death, and touched heaven while standing on the earth.
text.KJV: 18:16 And brought thine vnfained commandement as a sharpe sword, and standing vp filled all things with death, and it touched the heauen, but it stood vpon the earth.
text.DRV: 18:16 a sharp sword carrying thy unfeigned commandment, and standing filled all with death, and reached even to heaven standing on the earth.

text.Base: For they ranged like horses, and leaped like lambs, praising you, O Lord, who delivered them.
text.KJV: 19:9 For they went at large like horses, and leaped like lambes, praising thee O Lord, who hadst deliuered them.
text.DRV: 19:9 For even as horses they fed on meat, and as lambs they rejoiced, magnifying thee o Lord, which didst deliver them.

text.Base: For the elements changed places with one another, as on a harp the notes vary the nature of the rhythm, while each note remains the same.
text.KJV: 19:18 For the elements were changed in themselues by a kind of harmonie, like as in a Psaltery notes change the name of the tune, and yet are alwayes sounds, which may well be perceiued by the sight of the things that haue beene done.
text.DRV: 19:18 For the things of the field were turned into things of the water: and what soever were swimming things passed into the land.

text.Base: For in everything, O Lord, you have exalted and glorified your people, and you have not neglected to help them at all times and in all places.
text.KJV: 19:22 For in all things, O Lord, thou didst magnifie thy people, and glorifie them, neither didst thou lightly regard them: but didst assist them in euery time and place.
